overview: Hidden Strings is a five-color Pioneer control deck that leverages early removal, land acceleration, and board-clearing value spells to outlast opponents. By stabilizing with Fatal Push and untapping lands via Lotus Field, the deck transitions into a late-game grind where Vibrance provides both board control and massive card advantage. The strategy focuses on maintaining tempo while systematically depleting the opponent's resources.
win_conditions: Outdraw and outvalue opponents through Vibrance card draw
Stabilize early with Fatal Push and race with midrange threats
Use Lotus Field to accelerate into late-game finishers and sweepers

keep_criteria: Hands with Fatal Push and two lands
Low-cost threats that can race early
Hands containing Lotus Field for mana acceleration
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (preferably shock/fetch capable), at least one early interaction (Fatal Push, Thoughtseize, or Duress), and a Lotus Field or Vibrance if paired with a land.
Keep hands with 1-2 lands if they contain multiple early interaction spells or a clear combo piece with a land.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking removal or early interaction
Hands with three or more lands and no spells
Hands missing Fatal Push against aggressive matchups
Mulligan on 3+ lands with no early interaction, or 4+ lands with no lands.

early_game: Play lands efficiently and hold Fatal Push for instant-speed answers
Prioritize removing power 2 or less threats to stabilize
Use Lotus Field cautiously to avoid mana screw while setting up acceleration
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Thoughtseize/Duress to disrupt opponent's key pieces (target combo pieces, fast mana, or early threats).
If you have Fatal Push and a target, hold it for removal or discard.
Turn 2: Develop mana with shock lands/fetches, cast early interaction or a low-cost threat.
If you have Lotus Field + a land, play Field, then Vibrance (or vice versa) to untap it, setting up infinite mana.
Prioritize untapped lands for shock costs if needed.
mid_game: Develop board presence with resilient creatures
Sacrifice Lotus Field to untap lands for key spells or sweepers
Begin drawing into late-game resources while maintaining board control
Preserve hand disruption for key turns
Manage mana carefully to ensure you can untap Lotus Field with Vibrance.
Use fetch lands to dig for combo pieces or interaction.
Graveyard is a resource for Fatal Push and discard
late_game: Cast Vibrance to clear opponent's board and draw into finishers
Leverage card advantage to outlast opponent's resources
Close out games with large threats or repeated board clears
Infinite mana from Lotus Field + Vibrance allows you to cast a high-impact spell (like a massive creature, storm combo, or direct damage) or chain multiple removal spells.
Sometimes you can combo off with other cards in the deck.
Go all-in when you have Lotus Field + Vibrance on the board, enough mana to cast your win con, and no immediate threat to your combo pieces.
Also commit if you're ahead on board and can close the game with a single high-impact spell.

key_cards.name: Lotus Field
key_cards.notes: Sacrifice to untap lands for big spells; manage tapped entry carefully to avoid mana shortages

key_cards.name: Vibrance
key_cards.role: Board clear and card draw engine
key_cards.notes: Hold for opponent's big turns; synergizes with +1/+1 counter creatures if present

key_cards.name: Fatal Push
key_cards.notes: Use on power 2 or less threats to stabilize; prioritize opponent's aggressive creatures

sequencing_tips: Hold Fatal Push for instant speed to counter aggressive pushes
Time Lotus Field sacrifices to ensure you have enough untapped mana for key spells
Cast Vibrance after opponent commits resources to the board to maximize card draw and board state swing
Lotus Field enters tapped, so you need a land in play to untap it with Vibrance.
Don't play Vibrance without a target land.
Fatal Push is versatile but can be a dead draw against non-black creatures
consider holding it for discard if needed.
Avoid overcommitting to the board without interaction.
Watch for opponent's sideboard hate like Rest in Peace, Leyline of the Void, or artifact hate.

matchups.advice: Prioritize Fatal Push on their biggest threats; use Lotus Field to untap lands for early interaction
Race with midrange threats; use Vibrance to clear their board and draw into answers
Disrupt early setup with Fatal Push; use Lotus Field to accelerate into answers

Use discard to remove counterspells before they're cast. Chain interaction (Thoughtseize into Fatal Push) to break through. Use mana generation to cast multiple spells or use instant-speed combo pieces.
Apply early pressure with discard and removal. Don't let them stabilize; use Fatal Push on key ramp creatures. Sideboard in additional disruption and hate.

Win the interaction war. Prioritize Thoughtseize/Duress to remove their combo pieces. Protect your Lotus Field/Vibrance with counterspells or discard. Sideboard in additional disruption and artifact hate.

common_threats: Aggressive creatures with power 2 or less
Watch for opponent's discard targets (keep your combo pieces hidden).
Track their removal count (Fatal Push, Lightning Strike, etc.).
Note if they're playing graveyard hate or artifact hate.
Watch for combo indicators (fast mana, specific win cons).
